meta.title: graph-os: Product Architecture
meta.description: 3 naming options for the Kingly product ecosystem — from separate brands to full platform play

props.title: graph-os
props.subtitle: The operating system for AI agents — 3 paths forward

props.content: Two apps built. Domains acquired. Infrastructure proven. Now the question: how do ClawBuddy, ClawTok, kingly.cloud, Lev, and AgentPing come together as one product story? Three options — from conservative to category-defining.

props.label: INVENTORY
props.title: Domains, Apps & Infrastructure
props.subtitle: What's built, what's bought, what's proven

props.title: Domains Acquired
props.content:
**graph-os.com** — platform umbrella
**graph-os.ai** — enterprise manifestation
**graph.store** — federated marketplace
**kingly.cloud** — inference router

All registered. None pointed yet.

props.title: ClawBuddy (apps/frey-market)
props.content: Next.js 16.2 marketplace. Netflix-style gold/black UI. **67 routes, 185 pages, 16,500+ LOC.** Supabase backend live. 7 feature specs drafted. Compare + Trailers = next sprint.

props.title: ClawTok (apps/claw-tok)
props.content: Swift 6.0 / SwiftUI iOS app. **47 files, 14,800+ LOC.** 14 AI videos, 10 music tracks, 10 tool screens. Discover categories SHIPPED. Supabase services just landed. Purple+Gold brand.

props.title: kingly.cloud (plugins/claw-router)
props.content: Multi-provider inference gateway seed. **18 files, 1,662 LOC.** OpenAI-compatible API. Model aliasing. x402 crypto payments. Routing is decorative — needs real logic. Architecture doc: 408 lines, 5-phase build plan.

props.title: Lev (open source runtime)
props.content: Universal agent runtime. Constraint engine. FlowMind graphs. Entity graph compositor. **944 entities tracked**, 61 specs, 106 completed plans. graph.dna.yaml encodes the physics. core/graph/ IS the graph-os kernel.

props.title: AgentPing (community/agentping)
props.content: Human-in-the-loop protocol. **416 components migrated**, Studio GUI, daemon, MCP server, Chrome extension, WebMCP marketplace. Trust signals, uptime monitoring, capability badges.

props.label: OPTION A
props.title: Separate Brands — Status Quo+
props.subtitle: Two apps, shared backend, no unified story

props.content:
ClawTok stays as the iOS content app. ClawBuddy stays as the web marketplace. They share kingly.cloud for inference but are otherwise independent products with separate brands, separate user bases, and separate App Store listings.

**community/kingly-assistant** (OpenClaw fork) powers an AI assistant inside ClawBuddy. graph-os.ai and graph.store are parked for future use.

This is the safest path. Ship both apps independently. Cross-promote later. No renaming, no architectural entanglement.

props.title: ClawTok
props.content:
iOS app
AI content social feed
Creator tools
Standalone brand

props.title: kingly.cloud
props.content:
Shared inference
Invisible to users
Just the pipes

props.title: ClawBuddy
props.content:
Web marketplace
+ iOS companion app
OpenClaw assistant

props.title: Strengths
props.content: Simplest execution. No renaming. Each app has clear identity. Ship faster with smaller scope. Two chances at App Store discovery. Independent failure modes.

props.title: Weaknesses
props.content: No unified brand story. Hard to cross-sell. 'Why are these related?' Leaves graph-os.ai on the table. No enterprise angle. Two marketing budgets. No network effects between apps.

props.label: OPTION B
props.title: Unified Under ClawBuddy
props.subtitle: One brand, one app, one store — ClawTok becomes ClawBuddy iOS

props.content:
Rename ClawTok to ClawBuddy. The iOS app becomes **ClawBuddy** — your AI companion that combines content discovery (the feed), the marketplace (browse/compare/buy agents), creator tools (generate, remix), and an AI assistant.

The web marketplace at frey-market becomes **graph.store** — the marketplace URL, powered by the same backend. ClawBuddy Store.

**"ClawBuddy" = the buddy.** It watches AI content with you, helps you find agents, assists with creation. One brand, one App Store listing, one story: *"Your AI companion for everything."*

graph-os.ai is parked or becomes the developer portal / about page. kingly.cloud stays as the inference layer, visible to power users as "Powered by kingly.cloud."

props.title: ClawBuddy App
props.content:
iOS (was ClawTok)
Feed + Store + Assistant
One download, everything

props.title: graph.store
props.content:
Web marketplace
(was frey-market)
ClawBuddy's web face

props.title: kingly.cloud
props.content:
Inference layer
Visible to power users
"Powered by" badge

props.title: Strengths
props.content: One brand to market. 'ClawBuddy' has legs — buddy = companion = assistant. TikTok features make it sticky, marketplace makes it monetizable. One App Store listing with massive feature set. graph.store as the web URL is memorable.

props.title: Weaknesses
props.content: iOS app scope balloons — feed + store + assistant + creator tools. Harder to ship fast. Risk of 'trying to be everything.' No enterprise story. graph-os.ai still unused. The 'buddy' brand might not scale to enterprise.

props.label: OPTION C
props.title: graph-os.ai — The Full Platform Play
props.subtitle: Enterprise white-label + consumer app + federated marketplace + open-source runtime

props.content:
**graph-os.ai is THE platform.** It's what Lev becomes when it grows up. The operating system for AI agents — open-core, constraint-enforced, graph-native.

**graph.store** is the federated marketplace protocol. Anyone can run a graph.store node. Your flagship instance is the main marketplace. Enterprises run `company.graph.store` backed by the same protocol. The graph IS the marketplace — every agent is a node, every capability is an edge, compare = subgraph diff, discovery = graph walk.

**ClawBuddy** is the consumer face. Fun, social, accessible. Combines content + store + assistant. Built ON graph-os, not AS graph-os.

**kingly.cloud** is BOTH infrastructure (powers everything under the hood) AND a standalone product (developers buy API access, enterprises get private inference pools, white-label available).

The mesh node concept: run a node, serve inference, earn credits. `lev deploy --mesh` — agents self-register to the graph, inference routes through kingly.cloud, marketplace updates automatically.

**graph-os.ai = one of Lev's enterprise manifestations.** Lev is open source. graph-os.ai is the managed, SLA-backed, dashboarded version that enterprises pay for.

graph TD
    GOS["graph-os.ai\nTHE PLATFORM"] --> LEV["Lev Runtime\nOpen Source"]
    GOS --> KC["kingly.cloud\nInference Gateway"]
    GOS --> AP["AgentPing\nTrust Protocol"]
    GOS --> GS["graph.store\nFederated Marketplace"]
    
    GS --> CB["ClawBuddy\nConsumer App"]
    GS --> WL["White-Label\nEnterprise Nodes"]
    GS --> MESH["Mesh Nodes\nEarn Credits"]
    
    KC --> STANDALONE["Standalone API\nDeveloper SaaS"]
    KC --> EMBEDDED["Embedded\nPowers ClawBuddy"]
    KC --> PRIVATE["Private Pools\nEnterprise"]
    
    CB --> FEED["Social Feed\nwas ClawTok"]
    CB --> STORE["Store Tab\ngraph.store mobile"]
    CB --> ASSIST["AI Assistant\nkingly.cloud powered"]
    CB --> CREATE["Creator Tools\nGenerate + Remix"]

props.title: For Consumers: ClawBuddy
props.content: Download the app. Watch AI content. Browse the store. Get an AI buddy that helps you find, compare, and use agents. Free tier with ads, premium with kingly.cloud credits.

props.title: For Developers: kingly.cloud + Lev
props.content: Open-source Lev runtime. kingly.cloud API for inference. Publish agents to graph.store. AgentPing for trust signals. `lev deploy --mesh` to join the network.

props.title: For Enterprise: graph-os.ai
props.content: Managed graph-os dashboard. Private graph.store nodes. Private kingly.cloud inference pools. Constraint enforcement. Audit trails. SLA. lev-forge CI/CD.

props.title: For Creators: ClawBuddy Studio
props.content: Generate content with any AI tool via kingly.cloud. Publish to the feed. Monetize via credits. Analytics dashboard. Collab tools. Cross-post to X/TikTok/YouTube.

kingly.cloud becomes three things at once: infrastructure for your own apps (ClawBuddy uses it), standalone SaaS product (developers pay for API access), and enterprise white-label (companies run their own inference pool). That triple-dip is the actual business model.

props.title: Strengths
props.content: Category-defining positioning. Competes at platform level. Enterprise revenue path. Federated = network effects. 'graph-os' is unique and memorable. Every domain gets used. All existing code maps cleanly. Open-core model proven (GitLab, Supabase, PostHog).

props.title: Weaknesses
props.content: Most complex to execute. Enterprise sales cycle is long. The graph needs to actually work as described (not just internal plumbing). Federation is architecturally hard. Risk of over-positioning before the product proves itself. 'OS' overpromises.

props.label: EXECUTION
props.title: Recommended Path: Ship B, Grow Into C

items.title: Rename ClawTok → ClawBuddy iOS
items.description: Bundle ID, App Store listing, splash screen, icon. Same code, new name. Point graph.store → frey-market.

items.title: Ship Compare + AI Assistant
items.description: Compare tray in graph.store. Assistant bubble in ClawBuddy iOS. Both powered by kingly.cloud.

items.title: AgentPing + Store tab in iOS
items.description: Trust signals on marketplace. Store tab inside ClawBuddy iOS app. Cross-sell begins.

items.title: kingly.cloud standalone launch
items.description: Developer portal. API keys. Usage dashboard. Pricing tiers. OpenRouter competitor.

items.title: graph-os.ai developer portal
items.description: Platform docs. 'What is graph-os?' page. Lev + kingly.cloud + AgentPing under one umbrella.

items.title: Federation: graph.store protocol
items.description: Anyone can run a graph.store node. Agent registry protocol. Mesh inference routing.

items.title: Enterprise: graph-os.ai managed
items.description: Dashboard. Private nodes. SLA. Compliance. First enterprise design partner.

props.content: Ship Option B now, grow into Option C. The rename is cheap. The architecture is the same code. The brand consolidation pays off immediately. As federation + enterprise features land, graph-os emerges organically from what you've already built.

**Day 1:** Rename ClawTok → ClawBuddy. Point graph.store → frey-market. Ship.

**Month 1:** ClawBuddy has feed + store + assistant. kingly.cloud powers generation. AgentPing provides trust.

**Month 3:** "What's graph-os?" becomes a real question. The answer: it's what powers all of this.

**Month 6:** Enterprise wants private graph.store nodes. graph-os.ai is the answer. Option C has arrived — not because you built it all at once, but because you grew into it.

props.title: Option A
props.content:
clawtok.app → iOS app
clawbuddy.com → marketplace
kingly.cloud → inference
graph-os.ai → parked
graph.store → parked

props.title: Option B
props.content:
clawbuddy.com → iOS + web
graph.store → marketplace URL
kingly.cloud → inference
graph-os.ai → about page

props.title: Option C
props.content:
graph-os.ai → platform portal
graph.store → federated marketplace
clawbuddy.com → consumer app
kingly.cloud → inference SaaS
